analysis_result: n = 16 high-grade MPNSTs were included in the methylation-based high-grade MPNST subclustering, split evenly between the two methylation clusters: MPNST_G1 (n=8) and MPNST_G2 (n=8). This exactly matches the paper's reported count.
denominator_used: N = 16; definition: samples in 41467_2023_38432_MOESM4_ESM.xlsx 'Clinical Data' sheet where Tumor Type == 'MPNST' (i.e., high-grade MPNST, excluding 'Low-Grade MPNST') AND Methylation_Group is one of {'MPNST_G1', 'MPNST_G2'}. All 16 high-grade MPNST samples in the dataset have Methylation == 'X' (methylation data available) and each is assigned to either MPNST_G1 or MPNST_G2.
assumptions_made: Per the data context note, 'High-grade MPNST' = 'MPNST' Tumor Type (not 'Low-Grade MPNST'); Methylation_Group values 'MPNST_G1' and 'MPNST_G2' are the two MPNST methylation clusters referenced in the paper; samples with Methylation column value 'X' indicate methylation array data was generated and used in clustering; all 16 high-grade MPNSTs had methylation data available (no exclusions for missing data).
step_by_step_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
  - File: 41467_2023_38432_MOESM4_ESM.xlsx, sheet 'Clinical Data'
  - Shape: 108 rows x 15 columns
  - Relevant columns: Tumor_ID, Tumor Type, Methylation_Group, Methylation

B. COHORT DEFINITION
  - Filter 1: Tumor Type == 'MPNST' (high-grade MPNST, excluding 'Low-Grade MPNST') -> 16 samples
  - Filter 2: Methylation_Group in {'MPNST_G1', 'MPNST_G2'} -> 16 samples (all high-grade MPNSTs assigned to one of the two MPNST methylation clusters)
  - Final analytic cohort: N = 16

C. VARIABLE CODING
  - Tumor Type distribution (full dataset n=108): Neurofibroma=35, Cutaneous_NF=33, Atypical_NF=21, MPNST=16, Low-Grade MPNST=3
  - Methylation_Group distribution (full dataset): PremalignantNF_G3=29, Cutaneous NF_G7=21, Benign_NF-G4=20, Cutaneous NF_G6=12, Sporadic Spinal NF_G5=10, MPNST_G2=8, MPNST_G1=8
  - Methylation column: all 108 samples have value 'X' (methylation data present)
  - 'High-grade MPNST' mapped to Tumor Type == 'MPNST' per the data context note; 'Low-Grade MPNST' tumors (n=3) are NOT counted as high-grade

D. COMPUTATION
  - Subset dataset to Tumor Type == 'MPNST': yields 16 samples
  - Tabulate Methylation_Group among these 16: MPNST_G1 = 8, MPNST_G2 = 8
  - All 16 high-grade MPNSTs fall in one of the two MPNST methylation clusters; 0 in any other Methylation_Group
  - Conversely, all samples with Methylation_Group in {'MPNST_G1', 'MPNST_G2'} have Tumor Type == 'MPNST' (none are Low-Grade MPNST or other types)

E. RESULT
  - n = 16 high-grade MPNSTs were included in the methylation-based high-grade MPNST subclustering
  - Distribution: MPNST_G1 = 8, MPNST_G2 = 8
  - This exactly matches the paper's reported n = 16 (CONCORDANT)

F. CAVEATS
  - Assumes 'MPNST' in the Tumor Type column corresponds to high-grade MPNST as stated in the data context note
  - Verified all 16 high-grade MPNST samples have methylation data (Methylation == 'X') and are assigned to either MPNST_G1 or MPNST_G2 (no missing data, no exclusions)
  - No evidence of any high-grade MPNSTs being excluded from the subclustering analysis
